page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.18xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 72 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8163 processors running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated CPU resource allocation without hyperthreading.

The instance is equipped with 288.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met through attached network storage. For network connectivity, the instance offers a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps.

Due to its dedicated CPU allocation and high core count, this instance provides predictable compute performance for enterprise workloads. The balanced CPU-to-RAM ratio makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for general-purpose workloads requiring high parallel processing capabilities without the extreme memory demands of memory-optimized configurations. Suitable workloads include web serving, application servers, container orchestration, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.18xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features 72 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, powered by Intel Xeon 8163 processors running at 2.5 GHz with a single thread per core. The instance provides 288.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying on network-attached storage for data persistence. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 33 Gbps. With its dedicated CPU allocation and high core count, this instance is designed for predictable performance. It is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, application hosting, and containerized deployments that require substantial parallel processing power without the specialized resource ratios of compute- or memory-optimized instances.
